body.title: Consequence check: intention+why+observation unit + reliability ladder
body.description:
**What changed:** Adopted intention+why+observation as the atomic unit (closed loop), the reliability ladder (harness holds control flow), Why as a generative-rule multiplier, and the always-on-observer-as-standing-half — spawning a skills-why convention + auditor, the observer-pattern distillation, and offload harness-gates.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) The `Why:` convention could ossify into box-ticking — whys written to pass the auditor, not to capture real mechanism (the wrong-why-worse-than-none failure). (2) The skills-why auditor's 'incoherent why' escalation could quietly start calling inference on near-clean skills, breaking the cheap-filter cost guarantee. (3) Harness-driven step injection could over-constrain CC, making it brittle on legitimately novel steps the checklist didn't anticipate. (4) De-dup pressure could collide with T-why-sorter (constraints/patterns) as both grow toward the same observer contract.
**Signals to watch:** auditor inference-call count creeping above ~0 on clean runs; skills with `Why:` lines that are tautological/empty; CC retry-loops triggered by gates on valid steps; duplicated observer logic across T-why-sorter and T-always-on-observer-pattern.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-07-03 or when T-skill-why-auditor + T-offload-harness-gates ship.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
